summary: The project has resolved 81 of 118 tracked issues (69%), with most problem sets fully closed; remaining work is concentrated almost entirely in three problems (q4, q5, q6), which account for all 37 open issues. Solvability scores reveal a sharp bimodal split: q7 and q10 are highly tractable (57-58%) while most others languish below 20%, suggesting effort should be reallocated toward the highest-yield targets and away from intractable ones.
problems: q4 is the single largest blocker with 22 open issues — nearly 60% of all remaining open work.
q5 has 14 open issues with only 10 resolved, the worst resolved ratio of any active problem, and a low 14% solvability score indicating fundamental difficulty.
q6 has 1 lingering open issue despite 15 resolved — likely a stubborn edge case or verification gap blocking final closure.
Low solvability scores (q3 at 13%, q8 at 9%, q5 at 14%) signal several problems may be effectively unsolvable with current strategies.
Today's usage was minimal (only 2 runs, $0.19) — momentum has stalled and the project is under-utilizing available compute.
highlights: q7 (58%) and q10 (57%) have by far the highest solvability scores and represent the best near-term opportunities for new breakthroughs.
All AIM problem lists, Erdős problems, and first_proof_2 are fully resolved (0 open, 0 in-progress).
q6 is one resolved issue away from completion (15/16 done).
claude_opus_5-4 has produced consistently strong best-proof candidates (100 issues each) across all q1-q10, providing a solid baseline corpus.
Strong overall completion rate (69% resolved) demonstrates the pipeline is fundamentally working.

suggested_todos.title: Close out q6's single remaining open issue to fully resolve the problem
Launch a focused proof effort on q7 and q10, the two highest-solvability targets
Triage q4's 22 open issues to identify clusters and shared root causes before brute-forcing
Re-evaluate whether q3, q5, and q8 (sub-15% solvability) warrant continued investment or a strategy pivot
Increase daily run volume — current 2-run cadence is leaving compute budget unused
Audit q5's low resolved/open ratio to determine if proofs are failing verification or being abandoned

mistakes: Spreading effort thin across low-solvability problems (q3, q8) instead of concentrating on high-yield targets (q7, q10) — a misallocation of scarce attention.
Allowing q4 open issues to accumulate (22) without periodic triage, creating a backlog that obscures whether failures share a common cause.
Treating near-complete problems (q6 at 15/16) as done and leaving the final issue unaddressed — failing to capture easy closure wins.
Likely repeated reliance on a single model/strategy (claude_opus_5-4) across all problems without diversifying approaches for problems where solvability stalled below 20%.
Under-running the pipeline (2 runs today) suggests intermittent engagement; sporadic effort prevents iterative refinement that low-solvability problems require.

Probable confusion between 'best proof exists' (100 issues logged) and 'problem solved' — high proof counts on q1-q10 have not translated into resolved status, indicating verification gaps or proofs that don't fully satisfy hypotheses.
